010 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.marshall.csv.*;
020
021/**
022 * A pairing of a {@link CsvSerializer} and {@link CsvParser} into a single class with convenience to/of methods.
023 *
024 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
025 * <p class='bjava'>
026 *    <jc>// Serialize a list of beans to CSV</jc>
027 *    List&lt;Person&gt; <jv>people</jv> = List.of(<jk>new</jk> Person(<js>"Alice"</js>, 30), <jk>new</jk> Person(<js>"Bob"</js>, 25));
028 *    String <jv>csv</jv> = Csv.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>people</jv>);
029 *
030 *    <jc>// Parse CSV into a list of beans</jc>
031 *    List&lt;Person&gt; <jv>parsed</jv> = Csv.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>csv</jv>, List.<jk>class</jk>, Person.<jk>class</jk>);
032 *
033 *    <jc>// Parse CSV into a list of maps</jc>
034 *    List&lt;Map&lt;String,String&gt;&gt; <jv>rows</jv> = Csv.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>csv</jv>, List.<jk>class</jk>, Map.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
035 * </p>
036 * <p class='bjava'>
037 *    <jc>// Using instance methods</jc>
038 *    Csv <jv>m</jv> = Csv.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
039 *    <jv>csv</jv> = <jv>m</jv>.of(<jv>people</jv>);
040 *    <jv>parsed</jv> = <jv>m</jv>.to(<jv>csv</jv>, List.<jk>class</jk>, Person.<jk>class</jk>);
041 * </p>
042 *
043 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output (list of maps with a,b):</h5>
044 * <p class='bcode'>
045 *    a,b
046 *    foo,bar
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (list of beans with nested address, flattened):</h5>
050 * <p class='bcode'>
051 *    name,age,address_street,address_city,address_state,tags
052 *    Alice,30,123 Main St,Boston,MA,"a,b,c"
053 * </p>
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
056 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshallers">Marshallers</a>
057 * </ul>
058 */
059public class Csv extends CharMarshaller {
060
061   /**
062    * Default reusable instance.
063    */
064   public static final Csv DEFAULT = new Csv();
065
066
067   /**
068    * Constructor.
069    *
070    * <p>
071    * Uses {@link CsvSerializer#DEFAULT} and {@link CsvParser#DEFAULT}.
072    */
073   public Csv() {
074      this(CsvSerializer.DEFAULT, CsvParser.DEFAULT);
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Constructor.
079    *
080    * @param s
081    *    The serializer to use for serializing output.
082    *    <br>Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
083    * @param p
084    *    The parser to use for parsing input.
085    *    <br>Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
086    */
087   public Csv(CsvSerializer s, CsvParser p) {
088      super(s, p);
089   }
090}
